title: RV8GR boot sequence trace
purpose: Package the real RV8GR bring-up ROM start sequence so DP, PG, AC, Z, PC, and the final J-self loop are checked before physical single-step testing.

chips.role: Authoritative LI immediate path into AC and Z; child-port mapping is required before hierarchical execution

wiring.rule: T0/T1 fetch uses ROM as the DBUS owner for the boot bytes.
T2 immediate instructions use U34 as the only IBUS driver.
LOW for final J $06 only; PC loops at $0006.

behavior.setdp_80: First instruction initializes DP to $80 so RAM/register-page data access is safe.
behavior.setpg_00: Second instruction initializes PG to $00 so jumps and branches stay on ROM page 0 until changed.
behavior.li_00: Third instruction initializes AC to $00 and Z to 1.
behavior.j_self: Final instruction loads PC with {PG,$06}; because PG is $00, PC returns to $0006.
behavior.bus_policy: T0/T1 fetch bytes come from ROM; T2 immediate bytes come from U34. No trace vector allows multiple bus drivers.
behavior.edge_policy: U23, U32, U9, and the U1_U4 counter chain only update on their intended rising edges; opposite edges hold in this functional trace.

timing.clocking: 12 manual clock edges: four 2-byte instructions with T0/T1/T2 phases.
timing.frequency_boundary: Functional and manual single-step proof only; physical speed claims stay blocked until timing_margins.json evidence exists.
timing.deadband_boundary: Trace buses still require R/C settling and positive bus-owner deadband before physical signoff; this package records functional row order only.
timing.student_rule: After 12 clocks, DP=$80, PG=$00, AC=$00, Z=1, and PC is looping at $0006.

guarantees: SETDP $80 initializes DP before data access
SETPG $00 initializes PG before J self
J $06 loops to PC $0006 using the already-latched PG
T2 immediate bus ownership remains single-driver
The proof does not promote any physical clock-speed claim

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
